original: Galileo Galileipresents his telescope to the Venetian Senate in Venice
Astronomers announce the discovery of the most distant galaxy known, 4C41.17, a cluster of stars more than 10 billion light-years away

The original US "Dream Team" wins the basketball gold at the Barcelona Olympics 117-85 over Croatia, featuring superstarsMichael Jordan,Magic Johnson,Larry Bird,Scottie Pippen,Charles Barkley, andPatrick Ewing
Matthew Henson, American explorer, 1st African American to reach North Pole (Jun 4 1909), born in Nanjemoy, Maryland (d. 1955)
The Beatles are photographed by Iain MacMillan crossing the street as they walk away from EMI Recording Studios, for the cover of their "Abbey Road" album
The Kingsmen release "Louie, Louie;" some radio stations ban it due to rumors of obscene content
Svetlana Savitskaya, Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz T-7, T-12, T-15A, 2nd woman in space), born in Moscow, Soviet Union
Ernest Lawrence, American nuclear scientist and inventor (Cyclotron - Nobel 1939), born in Canton, South Dakota (d. 1958)
German airshipGraf Zeppelinbegins the first round-the-world flight by airship, departing from Lakehurst, New Jersey
Ernest Shackleton's ship "Endurance" leaves Plymouth, England, on the final major expedition of the Heroic Age of Antarctic Exploration
Japan launches Planet-A, a probe to Halley's Comet
Cornerstone is laid forTycho Brahe's observatory in Uraniborg, Denmark, which, when built, becomes the world's most advanced research institution
First known ascent in a hot-air balloon by Bartolomeu de Gusmão (indoors)
An EF2 tornado touches down in Kings County and Richmond County, New York State, the most powerful tornado in New York to date and the first in Brooklyn since 1889
Verne Gagne beats Fritz Von Erich in Amarillo to become NWA Champion
Nawal El Moutawakel of Morocco becomes the first female Olympic champion from a Muslim nation and the first from her country in the 400 m hurdles at the Los Angeles Olympics
Confederate submarine CSS H.L. Hunley is raised to the surface after 136 years on the ocean floor off the coast of South Carolina
Crete earthquake strikes with an estimated magnitude of 8, triggering a major tsunami that damages the Lighthouse of Alexandria and sweeps ships two miles inland in Egypt
Carl Lewiswins his third gold medal of the Los Angeles Olympics, joining Kirk Baptiste and Thomas Jefferson in an American sweep of the 200 m
Jacques Balmat and Michel Paccard are the first to climb to the top of Mont Blanc, the highest mountain in Western Europe
Ladislas Starevich, Polish animator, created stop-motion animation ("The Cameraman’s Revenge"), born in Moscow, Russia (d. 1965)
Mamoru Oshii, Japanese filmmaker and writer known for "The Ghost in the Shell", born in Tokyo, Japan
Renovated Central Park Zoo reopens after 4 years in New York City
Chicago White Sox wear shorts for a game instead of traditional baseball pants
Pioneer-Venus 2 with five atmospheric probes launches toward Venus
Brian Hyland's recording of "Itsy Bitsy Teenie Weenie Yellow Polkadot Bikini", a novelty pop song written by Paul Vance and Lee Pockriss, hits #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
Jostein Gaarder, Norwegian author on philosophy aimed at children (Sophie's World), born in Oslo, Norway
Spanish center forward Kiko scores twice as the home team wins the Olympic football gold medal with a 3-2 victory over Poland in front of 95,000 at Camp Nou, Barcelona
"Gangster's Paradise" single released byCoolio(Billboard Song of the Year, 1995)
Roger Penrose, English physicist (2020 Nobel Prize for Physics for work on black holes), born in Colchester, England
Germany goes through the field hockey competition undefeated to win gold with a 2-1 victory over Australia in the men's final at the Barcelona Olympics
US space shuttle STS-28 is launched
Bhutan, Land of the Dragon, becomes an independent monarchy
Corbett National Park and Tiger Reserve is founded in Uttarakhand, British India, as Hailey National Park (later renamed) as India's first national park
Esther Morris [Esther Hobart McQuigg], American suffragist, anti-slavery activist, and 1st female justice of the peace in the US, born in Tioga County, New York (d. 1902)
(Bennett) "Benny" Carter, American Grammy Award-winning jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, trumpeter, arranger, composer ("Harlem Renaissance Suite"), and bandleader, born in New York City (d. 2003)
Viorica Viscopoleanu, Romanian athlete (Olympic gold, long jump 1968), born in Storozhynets, Romania
The Edge [David Evans], British rock guitarist (U2 - "I Will Follow"; "Desire"; "Where The Streets Have No Name"), born in London
Dapper Dan [Daniel Day], American fashion designer, born in Harlem, New York
Louis Dunbar, American basketball player (Harlem Globetrotters), born in Minden, Louisiana
Esther Williams, American swimmer and actress (Dangerous When Wet), born in Inglewood California (d. 2013)
Gene Deitch, American illustrator (Tom Terrific, Nudnik), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2020)
Esma Redžepova, Macedonian Romani folk music vocalist, songwriter and humanitarian, known as "the Queen of the Gypsies", born in Skopje, Tsardom of Bulgaria (d. 2016)
José Jiménez, Puerto Rico-American civil rights leader, founder of the Young Lords in Chicago and co-founder of the Rainbow Coalition, born in Puerto Rico (d. 2025)[1]
Emilio Flores Márquez "Don Millo", Puerto Rican sugar cane worker and centenarian, (world's oldest man in 2021), born in Carolina, Pueto Rico (d. 2021)[1]

Lucky Millinder [Lucius Venable], American swing and rhythm-and-blues bandleader (Rosetta Tharpe - "Shout, Sister, Shout"; Wynonie Harris - "Who Threw The Whiskey in the Well"), born in Anniston, Alabama (d. 1966)
Cecil Travis, American baseball infielder (1st player to get 5 hits on debut; MLB All Star 1938, 40, 41; Washington Senators), born in Riverdale, Georgia (d. 2006)
10.75 inches (27.305 cm) of rainfall at Lockington Dam, Lockington, Ohio (state record)
Comet C/1864 N1 (Tempel) passes within 0.0964 AU (approximately 14.4 million km or 8.9 million miles) of Earth
Snow falls on Lake Michigan
MLB St. Louis Cardinals are 12 games back in the NL and go on to win the pennant
Texas Rangers turn their first triple play against the Athletics
MLB New York Yankees honorCasey Stengelby retiring his jersey number 37; Stengel manages the team from 1949 to 1960, winning 7 World Series titles in 10 appearances
Hamilton Tiger-Cats (CFL) beat Buffalo Bills (NFL) 38-21 in Hamilton, Ontario
Jarry Park is approved by Montreal Mayor Jean Drapeau for interim use by the Expos
China announces plans to close 23 rare earth mines and up to 50 smelting companies
India agrees to give Bhutan 32 square miles (82.88 square kilometers) of territory in the area of Dewangiri
Common Chiefs of Staff meet in Quebec
Hieronymus van Alphen, Dutch poet of children's poems (Proeve van kleine gedigen voor Kinder), born in Gouda, The Netherlands (d. 1803)
The US Army installs the first tricycle landing gear on the Army's Wright Flyer
